gaps: a corpus lookup that resolves a docket's linked cluster ids to citation/disposition data (the row was bare while the snapshot pointed at an opinion cluster)
an era/regime hint on the event (mandatory-jurisdiction vs discretionary-cert) so the predictor knows which disposition labels are reachable
helpful: fedcourts stats base rates as the calibration anchor for a bare snapshot
corpus.py's scope predicates documenting how pre-1925 cases are supposed to be excluded
notes: fedcourts query returned priors but relevance ranking had little to key on for this case (no topic, no dates, no docket number); direct sqlite inspection of the corpus row was needed to see why the scope gate missed it.
